fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 48-year-old Hispanic female with schizoaffective disorder with residual symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Martinez attended appointment with daughter who supervises medications. Daughter confirms mother takes quetiapine 300 mg twice daily via her observation, no missed doses. Despite reliable adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptoms. Reports persistent auditory hallucinations several times daily of woman's voice making critical comments. Endorses paranoid thoughts that neighbors spread rumors about her family. Sleep disrupted at 5 hours nightly. Appetite fair, weight stable at 173 lbs. Not working, stays home caring for grandchildren occasionally. Limited social activities outside home. Daughter reports mother increasingly isolated. Mood 'down and nervous' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Ricardo Lopez, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Middle-aged female, adequate grooming, appears anxious
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Auditory hallucinations, paranoid ideation about neighbors

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizoaffective Disorder with persistent symptoms and anxiety.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with daughter; not employed; provides childcare; socially isolated; ongoing symptoms.
fields.Author: Note written by Ricardo Lopez, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Familia Mental Health, 5623 Community Way, El Paso, TX 79901, Phone: (555) 883-4409, Fax: (555) 883-4410
